headline: MARKET WRAP — May 15
telegramText: MARKET WRAP — May 15, 2026 Markets closed with stark sector divergence as energy surged while metals faced broad-based selling pressure. The disconnect between crypto ETF inflows and miner weakness highlighted infrastructure headwinds beyond pure exposure plays. Tomorrow's session hinges on whether commodity weakness spreads to broader risk assets or remains sector-contained. CLOSING PRICES SPY -0.4% | QQQ -0.8% | VIX +6.8% | TLT -1.3% | DXY +0.4% | OIL flat vs MORNING SPY -1.8% | QQQ -2.6% | VIX +10.2% | TLT -1.3% | DXY -0.1% | OIL -1.3% EVENING NEWS ▲ Malaysia: Growth risks and steady rates – UOB ▲ China Allows Exports for 425 US Beef Plants, Trade Group Says ▼ THORChain exploit raises fresh concerns over MPC wallet security ▼ Gold sinks 2% as Iran war fuels fresh inflation shock ▲ US Dollar Index climbs to five-week high as hawkish Fed bets gather pace SCENARIO SHIFTS ↑ Late Cycle · ↓↓ Goldilocks · ↑ Stagflation · ↑ Hegemony REGIME CHECK Risk Gauge: 36.1/100 (Low). Regime: OVERHEATING. SECTOR CLOSE Top sectors: XLE +3.1%, XLF +0.2%, URA -6.3%, COPX -9.6%. Sector dispersion: 70 (EXTREME, breadth SELECTIVE_WEAKNESS), 25% positive 1w.
